reset_note: FULL WIPE — cleared every FUND/WATCH/KILL incl FPH; machine re-runs with zero memory

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: XBiotech — antibody biotech; a near-net-net cash box funded by the 2019/2020 Janssen bermekimab sale, owns its Austin manufacturing facility.
dossiers.thesis: XBIT trades at $2.30 vs $3.63/share hard net cash ($110.6M cash, ZERO financial debt, ~$4.8M non-tax liabilities) — ~63% of net cash. The market prices it as a cash-incinerating biotech after the Dec-2024 natrunixib Phase-II failure paused the rheumatology program, but it ignores a management team that has RETURNED ~$495M since 2019 via modified-Dutch tenders (Feb-2020, May-2023) and a $2.47 dividend (Jul-2021). The disciplined house knows the tender history is a proven, repeatable mechanism, not a hoped-for readout.
dossiers.why_mispriced: Post-clinical-failure neglect: a small, uncovered cash box whose lead pipeline just failed, so it is dumped by biotech holders at a fraction of countable cash despite zero debt and a facility it owns.
dossiers.catalyst: A modified-Dutch-auction tender or special dividend — the mechanism XBIT has used repeatedly to return the pile rather than burn it.
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: Within 18 months XBIT either announces a tender/dividend OR the price closes the gap to >=0.85x net cash; if neither and cash burns below $3.00/sh net, the neglect thesis is wrong.
dossiers.kill_condition: Net cash per share falls below $3.00 (burn without a capital-return action) or a going-concern / new dilutive raise appears.
dossiers.adversarial: The house knows the catalyst is DORMANT — no live tender/ATM/dividend in 2026 — and the lead program failed, so unlocking the discount is management-dependent with ~3yr of melt; realization risk, not floor risk, is the bet.
citations: natrunixib Phase-II failure / rheumatology pause, company release 2024-12-23

verification.notes: Primary 10-Q read: $115.46M cash, $0 financial debt, $4.82M total liabilities, 30.49M FD (options deep OTM). Net cash $3.63/sh. Cold-start blind verifier (task aa64/a47f XBIT).

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: Stratus Properties — Austin TX real-estate developer (entitled land, mixed-use, retail) in a stockholder-approved complete liquidation & dissolution.
dossiers.thesis: STRS trades at $28.51 vs management's stockholder-approved liquidation estimate of $29.73-$37.69/share — BELOW even the conservative low end. The board (3/24/26) and stockholders (6/1/26) approved a Plan of Complete Liquidation; a $5.00/share initial distribution (17.5% of price) pays 7/20/26, and Austin land carried at cost understates market. The market discounts the illiquid, non-SEC-reporting land wind-down after delisting (~8/10/26); the floor is a real, board-approved net-realizable estimate, not an asserted NAV.
dossiers.why_mispriced: Forced-seller / orphaned-security discount: an in-progress liquidation that will delist and deregister, leaving an untradeable dark stub — index and liquidity-constrained holders sell below realizable value.
dossiers.catalyst: The liquidating distribution schedule — $5.00/sh on 7/20/26, then serial payouts as Austin land and operating properties are sold (Jones Crossing already sold for $46.5M).
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: By 2028-06-30 cumulative liquidating distributions reach >=$29.73/share (the conservative estimate); if the running total tracks below $26/share, the land-at-cost floor was wrong.
dossiers.kill_condition: A revised liquidation estimate cuts the low end below $26/share, or a distribution is suspended/reversed.
dossiers.adversarial: The house knows the margin to the CONSERVATIVE estimate is thin (~4%), and after ~8/10/26 you hold an untradeable dark stub trusting a 6-month-stale, Austin-land-market-dependent estimate through lender/partner consents and reserve draws — a multi-year illiquid special situation, not a liquid position.
citations: STRS 8-K 'Initial Liquidating Distribution' filed 2026-07-01, accession 0000885508-26-000035 ($5.00/sh, record 7/13 pay 7/20)

verification.notes: Board+stockholder-approved liquidation; net debt $70.2M reconciled; assets transacting (Jones Crossing $46.5M). $5 distribution is only 17.5% — bulk of catalyst remains. Cold-start blind verifier (task a937 STRS).

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: Star Holdings — the 2023 iStar/Safehold spin-off holding the legacy non-ground-lease assets (a ~18.8% marked-to-market stake in Safehold/SAFE, plus land, operating real estate, and loans), in a declared monetization/wind-down; externally managed by Safehold (Jay Sugarman).
dossiers.thesis: STHO trades at $9.23 vs a wind-down NAV of ~$23/share. The DOMINANT asset is LIQUID and marked-to-market: ~13.5M SAFE shares (18.8% of Safehold) worth ~$221M, and SAFE net of its $92.8M margin loan is ~$10.64/share — already ABOVE the whole stock price — so the ~$225M book of land / operating RE / loans is priced at roughly zero. The market applies a permanent Sugarman/Safehold related-party discount, but the manager fee is stepping DOWN hard ($25M FY24 -> $15M FY25 -> $10M FY26 -> $7.5M -> 2.0% of book), the termination penalty rolls off 3/31/2027, and both debt facilities mature 3/31/2028 — a forcing function for monetization.
dossiers.why_mispriced: Declared wind-down / monetization vehicle with an external-manager conflict overhang and SAFE-price gearing via a margin loan — index and liquidity-constrained holders sell the levered stub below the value of its own liquid core holding.
dossiers.catalyst: Active monetization: land-development sales, mezz repayments (Asbury Park repaid in full), sub-NAV buybacks, a steeply declining fee schedule (termination penalty off 3/31/2027) and a 3/31/2028 debt-maturity wall forcing realization.
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: By 2028-06-30 STHO monetizes assets / buys back stock such that cumulative realization + residual NAV exceeds ~$14/share; if the SAFE-net-of-margin value per share falls below the price and no buyback/sale closes the gap, the thesis is wrong.
dossiers.kill_condition: SAFE-net-of-margin per share falls below the stock price AND the wind-down stalls (no asset sales/buybacks for two consecutive quarters), or a covenant/margin-call event on the 2028 facilities.
dossiers.adversarial: The house knows the overhang is real and must be sized around: Sugarman/Safehold controls the manager AND extends STHO's credit facility AND dictates how STHO votes its SAFE shares; the value is geared to SAFE's price through the margin loan; and it is a slow, levered wind-down with no distributions yet — a bounded convex stub, not a clean compounder. Size small.
citations: STHO 10-Q period 2026-03-31, filed 2026-05-08, SEC accession 0001953366-26-000010 (SAFE stake FV $183.0M @ $13.53/18.8%, debt maturities 3/31/2028, fee schedule + $55M termination fee, Safe voting restriction)
SAFE stake independently marked at market (SAFE $16.39, 2026-07-02)

verification.notes: Blind land-family verifier (rebuilt pipeline). SAFE stake is a liquid public equity marked to market (not asserted NAV); SAFE net of $92.8M margin > price. Net debt $161.4M reconciled full-stack. Active monetization + 2028 debt wall = catalyst. Sugarman/Safehold conflict priced-in overhang.
